mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter eases discounting and credit stress, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy is actively being cut.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns in periods where FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if triggered samples produce negative average returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent 6-month average marks labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign accuracy stays below 55% in any rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth in RSAFS is weak while housing starts in HOUST are also weak, household-demand softness signals earnings deceleration and the S&P 500 should fall over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST weakness state does not outperform each single series alone in predicting negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An improving INDPRO trend after prior weakness indicates stabilizing industrial activity and inventory turnover, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its value 1 month earlier after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if post-downturn INDPRO rebounds are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if mean forward return is not above zero in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A strong GDPC1 print relative to the prior quarter confirms aggregate real-demand growth, and that macro strength should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is positive versus the prior quarter and exceeds its previous 4-quarter median
failure_condition: Reject if positive-quarter GDPC1 growth episodes do not produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect flips negative in stable growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS stays low, real rates fall and policy remains behind inflation, which should be bullish for equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS gap does not improve 20-day SPX returns relative to either series alone, or if the interaction loses significance when inflation is above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market weakening regime, rising PAYEMS growth relative to UNRATE indicates an employment surprise that supports risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at least 0.2 percentage points above its 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS surprises during elevated-UNRATE periods do not generate positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms PAYEMS alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST is a leading signal of weakening household demand and tighter credit conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days after sustained housing weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 3 consecutive months and is below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if sustained housing declines are followed by non-negative average 60-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate does not exceed 50% in any 3-year rolling window.
